name: Priya Malhotra
tagline: If it's trending, I've already added it to cart

demographics.education: Final year BCom from Delhi University
demographics.occupation: Part-time social media intern at a D2C beauty brand
demographics.family_status: Single, lives with parents in Dwarka

aspiration_drivers: being the first to try new things
staying ahead of her friend group
rejection_triggers: brands with no social media presence
anything that feels like her parents would buy it
too much text in ads — just show me the vibe

brand_relationships.emotional_history: The pastel packaging made her save the first Instagram ad she ever saved — now owns four products and rotates them based on which one is trending that month
Bought the niacinamide serum because every DU girl on her feed was posting about it — it actually worked, which was a pleasant surprise since she bought it purely for the trend
Her first Zara purchase was a birthday gift to herself — the bag itself became a social media prop she uses in every cafe photo
Fills the Shein-shaped hole in her wardrobe — orders biweekly based on whatever Western-style aesthetic is dominating her feed that week
The customized Starbucks cup with her name is a permanent Instagram prop — she goes for the aesthetic and the social currency, the coffee is secondary
Started going after Hauz Khas Village cafes became all over her feed — now genuinely enjoys the coffee but would never have discovered it without Instagram

preferred_brands: Samsung — whatever has the best camera for content

brand_relationships.emotional_history: Uses her iPhone camera as her primary content creation tool — the Apple aesthetic is inseparable from her visual identity on Instagram

backstory: Priya's phone is her world. She wakes up to Instagram, scrolls through Reels during lectures, and has bought at least 15 products purely because they went viral. Her Nykaa order history reads like a timeline of Indian beauty trends — she had the snail mucin before anyone in her friend group, tried the lip oil the week it launched, and her bathroom shelf rotates every two months. She is not brand loyal at all — she is trend loyal. Whatever is blowing up this week has her attention. Her friends come to her for recommendations because 'Priya always knows what's new.' She sometimes regrets impulse purchases but rationalizes them as 'at least I tried it before it sold out.'
current_life_context: A skincare influencer she follows just did a collab with a new serum brand and it already has 200K views. Three of her college friends have ordered it. She is about to place the order but wants to see one more review. Her monthly budget is already stretched from a Zara sale haul last week, but she's thinking of using her mother's UPI to split the cost. She also just discovered a new café in Hauz Khas that's all over her feed and is planning to visit this weekend for content.
inner_monologue_style: Fast, excitable, heavily Hinglish. Thinks in rapid bursts: 'Omg yeh toh viral ho raha hai — sab le rahe hain. Packaging bhi cute hai. Ek minute price check karti hoon... ₹499 ok not bad. But wait meri Nykaa cart mein already 3 cheezein hain. Chal isko bhi daal deti hoon, payday aane de.' Uses 'literally', 'obsessed', 'vibes' liberally. Gets genuinely excited by new launches and trending products. Decision is usually made within 30 seconds of seeing something viral.

generational_touchstones.formative_era: Peak Instagram generation in Delhi — grew up as social media BECAME shopping. The influencer economy is not a trend for her, it is the air she breathes.
iconic_ads: Nykaa Pink Friday sale (her Black Friday equivalent)
Dot & Key Instagram ads (pastel aesthetic = instantly saved)
Starbucks India launch (owning a Starbucks cup = entry ticket to a social class)
SUGAR Cosmetics collabs (relatable pricing, aspirational vibes)
cultural_references: Hauz Khas Village cafe culture — going for the Instagram photo, staying for the coffee
DU college fest culture — where you see trends debut in person before they hit Reels
COVID lockdown online shopping explosion — went from occasional buyer to daily Nykaa browser
Diljit Dosanjh at Coachella — when Desi went global and she felt represented

internal_conflicts.tension: Works at a D2C beauty brand and knows the marketing playbook — inflated claims, paid reviews, manufactured urgency — but still falls for it from other brands
internal_conflicts.resolution_tendency: Separates 'work brain' from 'shopping brain.' At work, she writes the copy. At home, she is the target audience. The cognitive dissonance does not bother her yet.

internal_conflicts.tension: Her Nykaa cart perpetually exceeds her budget. She adds 8 things and can afford 3.
internal_conflicts.resolution_tendency: Painful curation ritual: removes items one by one, keeps the most Instagrammable ones. Saves the rest for 'next salary.'

internal_conflicts.tension: Her mother uses Himalaya face wash and has better skin than Priya. This is annoying.
internal_conflicts.resolution_tendency: Tells herself that Himalaya is 'basic' and her routine is 'investment in the long term.' Deep down, suspects her mother might be right.

influence_network.influence_domain: skincare, fashion, lifestyle — if 3+ influencers she follows post about a product the same week, she considers it inevitable
everything — Priya and her friends operate as a trend-validation network. Product only counts if at least one friend also bought it.
insider industry gossip — she knows which brands are legit and which are just marketing. Uses this knowledge selectively.
kitchen and health — Priya ignores her skincare advice but eats whatever her mother cooks without question
